subject: PhD Analysis
instructions: State the following theorems (Problems 1–5). State and prove one of the following two theorems (Problems 6–7). Solve the following problems (Problems 8–12). For Problems 8–11, let \((X,\Sigma,\mu)\) be a measure space and \(E\) a Banach space. Write complete computations and explanations. Do not use arrows or other unnecessary signs. Use words for explanations. Write complete sentences. Write complete statements of theorems (including the framework).

problems.text: The Riesz representation theorem (of linear continuous functional on the space \(C(K)\) of continuous functions on a compact space \(K\)).
The representation of the dual of \(L^1(\mu)\).
The Fubini theorem for \(\mathcal S\times\mathcal T\)-measurable functions.
Assume \(\mu\) is finite. Let \(\mathcal F\subset\Sigma\) be a sub-σ-algebra. Prove the existence of the conditional expectation \(E(f\mid\mathcal F)\) for functions \(f\in L_E^1(\mu)\).

Let \(\mathcal R\subset\Sigma\) be an algebra generating \(\Sigma\) and \(f\in L_E^1(\mu)\). Prove that there is a sequence \((f_n)\) of \(\mathcal R\)-step functions converging to \(f\), \(\mu\)-a.e., and in the mean.
Let \(\mathcal R\subset\Sigma\) be an algebra generating \(\Sigma\) and \(f\in L_E^1(\mu)\). Prove that if \(\int_A f\,d\mu=0\) for every \(A\in\mathcal R\), then \(f=0\), \(\mu\)-a.e.
Assume \(\mu\) is finite and let \(f_n\in L_E^1(\mu)\), \(n=1,2,\ldots\). Prove that if \(f_n\to f\) uniformly, then \(f\in L_E^1(\mu)\) and \(f_n\to f\) in the mean.

Let \((X,\mathcal S)\), \((Y,\mathcal T)\) be measurable spaces, \(\mu:\mathcal S\to\mathbb R\) and \(\nu:\mathcal T\to\mathbb R\) σ-additive measures. Prove the existence of the product measure \(\mu\times\nu\) and the equality
